title: Performance, Capacity, Efficiency, and Cost Tradeoffs · Product Forensics
summary: A preserved decision record for performance, capacity, efficiency, and cost tradeoffs contains one plausible evidence, scope, design, implementation, evaluation, or ownership defect. Find the first unsupported claim before changing the product. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in capacity and cost evidence model and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evid…
objectives: Set task, response, interaction, payload, memory, CPU, queue, accessibility, and energy budgets tied to user conditions.
Model actors, operations, arrival patterns, concurrency, data sizes, locality, think time, bursts, failures, and growth.
Measure end-to-end and component behavior with controlled environments, percentiles, resource signals, variance, and profiler evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-2 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-2
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Performance Budget
steps.instruction:
debug case — A fast average hides slow devices, large payloads, p99 queue growth, inaccessible loading states, dependency quotas, and an unbounded bill. The debug variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Set task, response, interaction, payload, memory, CPU, queue, accessibility, and energy budgets tied to user conditions.

Inspect the Performance Budget trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Performance Budget.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for set task, response, interaction, payload, memory, cpu, queue, accessibility, and energy budgets tied to user conditions. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Performance Budget, keep Performance Budget connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For Performance Budget, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Inspect the failure record · Debug Performance Budget, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Performance Budget precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Inspect the failure record · Debug Performance Budget, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A fast average hides slow devices, large payloads, p99 queue growth, inaccessible loading states, dependency quotas, and an unbounded bill. The debug variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Set task, response, interaction, payload, memory, CPU, queue, accessibility, and energy budgets tied to user conditions.

For Inspect the failure record · Debug Performance Budget, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: One average latency target represents product performance.

stimulus.title: Performance Budget case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A fast average hides slow devices, large payloads, p99 queue growth, inaccessible loading states, dependency quotas, and an unbounded bill. The debug variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Set task, response, interaction, payload, memory, CPU, queue, accessibility, and energy budgets tied to user conditions.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: One average latency target represents product performance.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Set task, response, interaction, payload, memory, CPU, queue, accessibility, and energy budgets tied to user conditions. Artifact under review: capacity and cost evidence model.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the inspect the failure record evidence boundary.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Performance Budget, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
